data_source: OpenSky Network REST API (https://opensky-network.org/api). No authentication required for live state vectors. 1,070 aircraft tracks collected 2026-04-11 by polling /states/all across 5 European and North American airspace regions (Central Europe, NW Europe, France, NE USA, W USA). ~40 polls over ~10 minutes, 12s interval. ADS-B transponder data aggregated by OpenSky Network (Schäfer et al. 2014). Aircraft: 1,110 unique ICAO24 addresses observed; 1,070 with >=10 observations.
encoding_notes: Each ADS-B state vector (~15s interval) encoded as 3 characters: (1) ALTITUDE: baro_altitude (metres) -> feet -> 20 bins of 2,500ft each (0-50,000ft) -> A-T. (2) HEADING: true_track (0-360 deg) -> 8 compass octants (N/NE/E/SE/S/SW/W/NW) -> 0-7. (3) SPEED: velocity (m/s) -> knots -> 10 bins of 60kt each (0-600kt) -> a-j. Each flight is a separate sequence (pooled counting, no cross-flight concatenation). Observations deduplicated by timestamp (same aircraft visible in multiple region queries). HIGH structure expected: aircraft follow physics (limited turn/climb rates), follow airways (standard routes), obey ATC instructions. Strong per-channel autocorrelation: altitude/speed/heading all smooth over time.
